It belongs to the genus Neomammillaria, which is a group of small to medium-sized cacti. The plant's classification is closely related to other species in the Mammillaria subgenus, which is characterized by its small flowers and rounded stems. Neomammillaria fertilis is native to Mexico and is considered a rare and unique species within the cactus family.

intro: Neomammillaria fertilis, commonly known as the Fertilis Pincushion Cactus, is a species of cactus in the family Cactaceae, characterized by its small, globular stems covered in fine, sharp spines. The Fertilis Pincushion Cactus has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its purported health benefits, including its ability to aid in digestion, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Neomammillaria fertilis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which have been shown to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to support digestive health and immune function.
